title: Options 1
description: The `Option` type represents a value that might be absent. It is either `Some(value)` or `None`.
instructions: Fix the code to handle the Option return type properly.
hints: Option is an enum with two variants: Some(T) and None.
Return Some(value) when a valid value exists, None when it doesn't.
Fill in: None, Some(0), and Some(5) for each branch.
explanation: The `Option<T>` type is Rust's replacement for null values found in other languages. It forces you to explicitly handle the case where a value might not exist. `Some(value)` wraps a present value, while `None` represents absence. The compiler ensures you handle both cases before using the value.
